title: Catalent Inc. (CTLT) Economic Prospect Score — Consensus Analysis
description: Consensus economic prospect analysis of Catalent Inc. (CTLT), averaging independent scores from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 across competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment catalysts.
verdictDetail: Catalent was acquired by Novo Holdings A/S for $16.5 billion in late 2024 and operates as a privately held subsidiary. Therefore, it is no longer a publicly traded standalone entity, rendering independent economic prospect scoring irrelevant. The analysis reflects the absence of public equity prospects.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; financial metrics no longer publicly applicable for forward scoring.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; market share now benefits Novo Holdings rather than independent public shareholders.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; pricing dynamics are internal to Novo Holdings' strategic operations.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; product development serves the parent company's pipeline.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; independent moat evaluation is no longer applicable.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; independent moat evaluation is no longer applicable.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; regulatory positioning benefits the parent entity.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; capital base absorbed by Novo Holdings.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; no longer followed by public equity analysts.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; public sentiment is moot.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Company acquired and taken private; capital allocation decided by parent organization.

keyRisks: Acquisition completion removes public equity upside.
keyCatalysts: None, as the company is privately held.
None, equity value fully realized in acquisition.
methodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored CTLT at 0/100 and Opus at 11/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
